# services.mysql

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.mysql`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.mysql.configFile` | `absolute path` | Override the configuration file used by MySQL. By default, NixOS generates one automatically from {option}`services.mysql.settings`. |
| `services.mysql.dataDir` | `absolute path` | The data directory for MySQL. ::: {.note} If left as the default value of `/var/lib/mysql` this directory will automatically be created before the MySQL server starts, otherwise you are responsible for ensuring the directory exists with appropriate ownership and permissions. ::: |
| `services.mysql.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable MySQL server. |
| `services.mysql.ensureDatabases` | `list of string` | Ensures that the specified databases exist. This option will never delete existing databases, especially not when the value of this option is changed. This means that databases created once through this option or otherwise have to be removed manually. |
| `services.mysql.ensureUsers` | `list of (submodule)` | Ensures that the specified users exist and have at least the ensured permissions. The MySQL users will be identified using Unix socket authentication. This authenticates the Unix user with the same name only, and that without the need for a password. This option will never delete existing users or remove permissions, especially not when the value of this option is changed. This means that users created and permissions assigned once through this option or otherwise have to be removed manually. |
| `services.mysql.ensureUsers.*.ensurePermissions` | `attribute set of string` | Permissions to ensure for the user, specified as attribute set. The attribute names specify the database and tables to grant the permissions for, separated by a dot. You may use wildcards here. The attribute values specfiy the permissions to grant. You may specify one or multiple comma-separated SQL privileges here. For more information on how to specify the target and on which privileges exist, see the [GRANT syntax](https://mariadb.com/kb/en/library/grant/). The attributes are used as `GRANT ${attrName} ON ${attrValue}`. |
| `services.mysql.ensureUsers.*.name` | `string` | Name of the user to ensure. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.clusterAddress` | `string` | Full Galera cluster connection string. If nodeAddresses is set, this will be auto-generated, but you can override it with a custom value. Format is typically 'gcomm://node1,node2,node3' with optional parameters. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.clusterPassword` | `string` | Optional password for securing cluster communications. If provided, it will be used in the clusterAddress for authentication between nodes. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable MariaDB Galera Cluster. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.localAddress` | `string` | IP address or hostname of this node that will be used for cluster communication. Must be reachable by all other nodes. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.localName` | `string` | The unique name that identifies this particular node within the cluster. Each node must have a different name. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.name` | `string` | The logical name of the Galera cluster. All nodes in the same cluster must use the same name. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.nodeAddresses` | `list of string` | IP addresses or hostnames of all nodes in the cluster, including this node. This is used to construct the default clusterAddress connection string. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.package` | `package` | The MariaDB Galera package that provides the shared library 'libgalera_smm.so' required for cluster functionality. |
| `services.mysql.galeraCluster.sstMethod` | `one of "rsync", "mariabackup"` | Method for the initial state transfer (wsrep_sst_method) when a node joins the cluster. Be aware that rsync needs SSH keys to be generated and authorized on all nodes! |
| `services.mysql.group` | `string` | Group account under which MySQL runs. ::: {.note} If left as the default value this group will automatically be created on system activation, otherwise you are responsible for ensuring the user exists before the MySQL service starts. ::: |
| `services.mysql.initialDatabases` | `list of (submodule)` | List of database names and their initial schemas that should be used to create databases on the first startup of MySQL. The schema attribute is optional: If not specified, an empty database is created. |
| `services.mysql.initialDatabases.*.name` | `string` | The name of the database to create. |
| `services.mysql.initialDatabases.*.schema` | `null or absolute path` | The initial schema of the database; if null (the default), an empty database is created. |
| `services.mysql.initialScript` | `null or absolute path` | A file containing SQL statements to be executed on the first startup. Can be used for granting certain permissions on the database. |
| `services.mysql.package` | `package` | Which MySQL derivation to use. MariaDB packages are supported too. |
| `services.mysql.replication.masterHost` | `string` | Hostname of the MySQL master server. |
| `services.mysql.replication.masterPassword` | `string` | Password of the MySQL replication user. |
| `services.mysql.replication.masterPort` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port number on which the MySQL master server runs. |
| `services.mysql.replication.masterUser` | `string` | Username of the MySQL replication user. |
| `services.mysql.replication.role` | `one of "master", "slave", "none"` | Role of the MySQL server instance. |
| `services.mysql.replication.serverId` | `signed integer` | Id of the MySQL server instance. This number must be unique for each instance. |
| `services.mysql.replication.slaveHost` | `string` | Hostname of the MySQL slave server. |
| `services.mysql.settings` | `attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string) or a list of them for duplicate keys)` | MySQL configuration. Refer to <https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/5.7/en/server-system-variables.html>, <https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/8.0/en/server-system-variables.html>, and <https://mariadb.com/kb/en/server-system-variables/> for details on supported values. ::: {.note} MySQL configuration options such as `--quick` should be treated as boolean options and provided values such as `true`, `false`, `1`, or `0`. See the provided example below. ::: |
| `services.mysql.user` | `string` | User account under which MySQL runs. ::: {.note} If left as the default value this user will automatically be created on system activation, otherwise you are responsible for ensuring the user exists before the MySQL service starts. ::: |
